title: acalypha reptans var. pygmaea
intro: Acalypha reptans var. pygmaea, also known as Dwarf Copper Leaf, is a small perennial herb belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which provide various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as the ability to reduce oxidative stress and improve overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Acalypha reptans var. pygmaea can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
